file.content:
# Review: `deepmind/963.lean` ‚ÄĒ ErdŇĎs Problem 963

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# ErdŇĎs Problem 963`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/963`.
- [ ] **Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.**
  - The module docstring (lines 19‚Äď25) contains only the title, reference link, and bibliography entry `[Er65]`. It does **not** include a plain-English description of the problem. The problem description appears only in the theorem docstring (lines 31‚Äď48). The checklist requires the module docstring itself to contain a brief description.
- [x] Optional bibliography: `[Er65]` entry is present.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos963 ... end Erdos963`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos963`).
- [x] `open Finset BigOperators` appears **before** the namespace block (line 27).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `IsDissociated` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`, lines 31‚Äď34).
- [x] No `noncomputable` needed (the definition is a `Prop`).
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] `IsDissociated` uses `UpperCamelCase` (it returns a `Prop`).
- [x] No local notation used (none needed). N/A.
- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for `IsDissociated`. This is a "consider" item so not strictly required, but a simple test (e.g., verifying a known singleton is dissociated) would add confidence.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_963` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` conventions.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 5]`.
- [x] Has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`, lines 38‚Äď48).
- [x] Docstring references the source paper `[Er65]`.

### `answer()` usage

The original problem asks "Is it true that f(n) ‚Č• ‚Ćälog‚āā n‚Ćč?" ‚ÄĒ this is a **yes/no question** (Case 1).

- [x] The theorem type is `answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ P` (Case 1 form).
- [x] `answer(sorry)` is correct since the problem is open (unsolved).
- [x] The RHS formalizes the question as originally asked: for every nonempty finite set A ‚äā ‚ĄĚ, there exists a dissociated B ‚äÜ A with |B| ‚Č• ‚Ćälog‚āā |A|‚Ćč. So `answer(True)` would mean "yes."
- [x] Quantifiers (`‚ąÄ (A : Finset ‚ĄĚ), ...`) appear **after** `answer(sorry)` in the RHS of `‚ÜĒ`, not as theorem parameters.

**Formalization correctness:** `Nat.log 2 A.card` computes ‚Ćälog‚āā |A|‚Ćč for natural numbers, matching the problem's ‚Ćälog‚āā n‚Ćč. `IsDissociated` correctly captures "all subset sums are distinct." The use of `Finset ‚ĄĚ` for finite subsets of ‚ĄĚ is appropriate. The formalization looks faithful to the source.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Has exactly one `@[category ...]`: `category research open`.
- [x] `research open` is a valid category value.
- [x] Problem is open (confirmed at erdosproblems.com) and proof body contains `sorry`.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Has exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 5`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number is listed.
- [x] Only one number, so ordering/duplicates are N/A.
- [x] `5` (Combinatorics) is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.
- **Note:** The source at erdosproblems.com classifies this as a "Number theory problem," and the reference paper is titled "Extremal problems in number theory." Adding `AMS 11` (Number theory) alongside `AMS 5` may be warranted: `@[category research open, AMS 5 11]`. This is a suggestion, not a strict violation.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by sorry` (multi-line form: `by` on line 52, `sorry` indented on line 53 ‚ÄĒ acceptable per checklist).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for the open problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A. No variants present. The problem is a single question; no multi-part structure needed.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `‚ąÄ`, `‚ąÉ`, `‚äÜ`, `‚ąß`, `‚ąą`, `‚ĄĚ`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (longest is line 52 at ~80 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] Not verified (requires `lake build`).

---

## Summary

**Overall: PASS with minor suggestions.**

The file is well-structured and conforms to nearly all checklist items. Two items to address:

1. **Module docstring missing plain-English description (¬ß3):** The module docstring should include a brief summary of the problem (e.g., "ErdŇĎs asked whether every finite set A ‚äā ‚ĄĚ of size n contains a dissociated subset of size at least ‚Ćälog‚āā n‚Ćč"). Currently the description lives only in the theorem docstring.

2. **Consider adding `AMS 11` (¬ß8):** The problem originates from a number theory paper and erdosproblems.com classifies it under number theory. `@[category research open, AMS 5 11]` would be more precise.

    46‚Üísubset $B \subseteq A$ with $|B| \geq \lfloor \log_2 n \rfloor$.
    47‚Üí

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# ErdŇĎs Problem 963

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/963](https://www.erdosproblems.com/963)

[Er65] ErdŇĎs, P., _Extremal problems in number theory_, 1965.
-/

open Finset BigOperators

namespace Erdos963

/--
A finset of real numbers is *dissociated* if all subset sums are distinct:
for any two subsets $S$ and $T$ of $B$, if $\sum_{b \in S} b = \sum_{b \in T} b$ then $S = T$.
-/
def IsDissociated (B : Finset ‚ĄĚ) : Prop :=
  ‚ąÄ S T, S ‚äÜ B ‚Üí T ‚äÜ B ‚Üí (‚ąĎ b ‚ąą S, b) = (‚ąĎ b ‚ąą T, b) ‚Üí S = T

/--
ErdŇĎs Problem 963 [Er65]:

Let $f(n)$ be the maximal $k$ such that in any set $A \subset \mathbb{R}$ of size $n$ there is a
dissociated subset $B \subseteq A$ of size $|B| \geq k$ (i.e., all subset sums of $B$ are
distinct). Is it true that $f(n) \geq \lfloor \log_2 n \rfloor$?

Equivalently: for every finite set $A \subset \mathbb{R}$ of size $n$, there exists a dissociated
subset $B \subseteq A$ with $|B| \geq \lfloor \log_2 n \rfloor$.

ErdŇĎs noted that the greedy algorithm shows $f(n) \geq \lfloor \log_3 n \rfloor$.
-/
@[category research open, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_963 : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÄ (A : Finset ‚ĄĚ), A.Nonempty ‚Üí
    ‚ąÉ B : Finset ‚ĄĚ, B ‚äÜ A ‚ąß IsDissociated B ‚ąß Nat.log 2 A.card ‚Č§ B.card := by
  sorry

end Erdos963
